title: The Still Waterfall
subtitle: Chapter 02 · The Wider Fractures
introduction: A deep mountain gorge where a thin waterfall spills over granite rocks. Focus on the pool surface.
description: A heavy river stone drops from the cliff rim, but the water spray rises and splatters the granite rocks exactly two seconds before the stone hits the pool surface.
observation: The water splashes outwards, soaking the granite gorge before the falling stone can make contact.
reconstruction: Carry the falling stone, the splash pool, and the wet granite canyon.
attunements: The granite stone is dry and suspended in mid-air, carrying high gravitational energy.
The water is fully displaced and rippling before receiving the impact.
The cliff side is wet, showing fresh moisture from early water spray.
revelation:
What was wrong: the water splattered across the canyon before the falling stone could reach the pool.

Why it mattered: the gorge was keeping the memory of the impact, predicting the water's displacement before gravity could conclude its work.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not there. Focus on the pool surface, not the falling stone.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Gravitational desynchronization detected. Splash impact waves precede stone contact.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE WATER SPRAY DISPERSES.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE GRANITE STONE IS DRY AND SUSPENDED. The stone is falling, but remains completely dry in mid-air.
THE WATER IS FULLY DISPLACED AND RIPPLING. The splash occurs before any solid objects break the water surface.
THE CLIFF SIDE IS ALREADY WET. Granite walls are covered in mist spray before the waterfall impact.
